ddls:
CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "product_line" TEXT /* Line of products to which it belongs */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the product */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status (e.g., in development, production, discontinued) */,
  "launch_date" TEXT /* Date when the product was launched */,
  "revision_number" TEXT /* Revision number to track product versions */,
  "manufacturer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the manufacturer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "specifications" (
  "spec_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each specification entry */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product it belongs to */,
  "specification" TEXT /* Specification name (e.g., Input supply voltage, Output current) */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum value allowed for the specification */,
  "typ_value" REAL /* Typical value for the specification */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum value allowed for the specification */,
  "units" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the specification */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes regarding the specification */,
  "tolerance" REAL /* Tolerance level for the specification */,
  "criticality" TEXT /* Indicator of how critical the specification is (e.g., High, Medium, Low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("spec_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_specifications_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "testing_protocols" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test entry */,
  "spec_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the specification being tested */,
  "test_description" TEXT /* Description of the testing protocol */,
  "test_date" TEXT /* Date when the test was conducted */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the test (e.g., Pass, Fail) */,
  "tester_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tester who performed the test */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Description of the environment in which the test was conducted */,
  "equipment_used" TEXT /* List of equipment used for the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_testing_protocols_tester_id FOREIGN KEY ("tester_id") REFERENCES testers ("tester_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_testing_protocols_spec_id FOREIGN KEY ("spec_id") REFERENCES specifications ("spec_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "testers" (
  "tester_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tester */,
  "tester_name" TEXT /* Full name of the tester */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the tester */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the tester in the organization */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the tester belongs */,
  "certification" TEXT /* Any certifications the tester holds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tester_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compliance_audits" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit entry */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product being audited */,
  "audit_date" TEXT /* Date of the audit */,
  "audit_result" TEXT /* Result of the audit (e.g., Compliant, Non-Compliant) */,
  "auditor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the auditor who conducted the audit */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or findings from the audit */,
  "audit_type" TEXT /* Type of audit (e.g., Internal, External) */,
  "standard_reference" TEXT /* Reference to the standard being audited against */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compliance_audits_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compliance_audits_auditor_id FOREIGN KEY ("auditor_id") REFERENCES auditors ("auditor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "auditors" (
  "auditor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each auditor */,
  "auditor_name" TEXT /* Full name of the auditor */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the auditor */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the auditor in the organization */,
  "certification" TEXT /* Certifications held by the auditor */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the auditor belongs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("auditor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the test that produced the result */,
  "result_value" REAL /* Value of the test result */,
  "result_units" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the result */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes regarding the result */,
  "environment_conditions" TEXT /* Conditions under which the test was conducted (e.g., temperature, humidity) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES testing_protocols ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history entry */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product whose specifications changed */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date of the change */,
  "previous_specification" TEXT /* Previous specification value */,
  "new_specification" TEXT /* New specification value */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who made the change */,
  "change_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the change in specification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_history_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_history_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for authentication */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, auditor, manager) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_specifications" (
  "product_spec_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the product-specification linkage */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product */,
  "spec_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the specification */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the specification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_spec_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_specifications_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_specifications_spec_id FOREIGN KEY ("spec_id") REFERENCES specifications ("spec_id")
)
